title: Level 209
wikitext: {{Custom Theme|color=150|linkcolor=180|font=Jost|bg=biosector.png|bgfilter=blur(5px)}} {{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 209: "Indoor Neighbourhood"}} {{Voting Box|Upvotes=2|Downvotes=0}} {{Class Habitable}} {{Audio|file=didyoujustaeiou.mp3|align=left|volume=0.5}} == Description == '''Level 209''', known as ''"Indoor Neighbourhood"'', is a network of interconnected subterranean chambers decorated to replicate modern suburban environments. Nothing within '''Level 209''' appears truly organic, as every single thing within the level is artificial. The ceilings and upper walls of these chambers are covered in wallpaper depicting a static, blue sky with faint clouds, while large industrial fluorescent lights are embedded overhead. These lighting systems operate on a strict 24-hour cycle to simulate daytime and nighttime hours. {{Blurimage|caption=A picture of a house within '''Level 209.'''.|file=biosector.png}} In certain areas of the level, faint and distorted background music can be heard coming from no visible source, often resembling nostalgic or slow instrumental tunes. The air throughout the level carries a mild, clean scent similar to a newly built home, while a soft, steady electrical hum from the ceiling grid serves as a quiet ambient backdrop. == The Park == The Park serves as the main trading area on '''Level 209''', the meeting point for the level's sustained communities. Visually distinct from the residential formats, this sector resembles an expansive, high-tech indoor plaza or a public botanical garden enclosed beneath a reflective ceiling structure that distorts the room's perspective. The Park is bordered by massive vertical walls covered in dense synthetic moss, creating a highly organized, pristine, and clean environment. {{Blurimage|caption=A section in The Park.|file=parksector.png}}Large circular openings are embedded in the ceiling, projecting beams of intense white light that perfectly mimic natural sunlight, illuminating concrete pathways, structured wooden decking, and heavy stone benches. Multiple trading outposts have been established along these pathways, turning the sector into a community center where resources are shared daily. Because this area is entirely secure and brightly lit at all hours, it serves as the main economic heart of the level, where independent wanderers and organized groups gather to trade items collected from the surrounding houses and plan safe travel routes through the level. == Houses == The houses found throughout '''Level 209''' look very modern from the outside, but once you step indoors, they actually look like pretty common, everyday suburban homes. Almost every single house shares the exact same interior layout, featuring standard rooms with plain walls and simple furniture setups. {{Blurimage|caption=A common living room found throughout the houses.|file=inside209.png}} Across the level, houses generally feel very empty, and appliances like televisions or radios will only show static when you turn them on. While the main kitchen counters and pantries are usually empty, wanderers can find plenty of hidden food. [[Object 1|Almond Water]] and [[Object 6|Moth Jelly]] are the most common; they are usually found in crawlspaces or behind furniture. == Entities == === The Star-Hearted === '''The Star-Hearted''' is a unique and docile creature that likely acts like a receptionist and a sort of caretaker for '''Level 209'''. He has a very distinct look, appearing as a smooth, white humanoid figure with large, pitch black circular eyes and black stripes painted down his face. He features a black four pointed star right in the middle of his chest, and his arms and legs are covered in a dark pattern resembling corrosion. Even though it looks like rust or a burn, this black corrosion is completely safe and cannot be transmitted to you. {{Blurimage|caption=A blurry picture of The Star-Hearted.|file=starhearted.png}} Whenever a wanderer tries to take a picture of him, their camera will experience a strange blur effect, making his figure look fuzzy. He speaks with a slightly distorted voice that sounds like a normal male human. He has total free access to every single corner and sector of the level to do his caretaker duties. He is incredibly patient and peaceful, making him very easy to talk to. Wanderers can ask him almost anything about '''Level 209''', its layouts, or where to find resources, and he will answer calmly. {{Collapse|initial='''''I noticed something'''''|final='''...'''|body=I don't know. Something about him feels different lately. The Star-Hearted still acts the same around everyone else. He walks through the streets, checks the houses, answers questions... same as always. Calm voice.... Same weird stare... Sometimes he enters empty houses and stays inside for hours. Sometimes I see him turning the lights on in homes nobody lives in anymore. And recently, he's been asking wanderers to stay here more often. He just says things like “You’re safe here.” At first it sounded comforting. Now it doesn't. The level itself feels different too. The sky looks darker than before. Everything feels... still. Yesterday I caught him standing in the middle of the street staring at the houses for almost twenty minutes without moving. I don't think he's maintaining this place anymore. [[Level 209/offset1|I think he's preparing it for something...]]}} == Communities == === Faulehood === * '''Population:''' 24 members. * '''Description:''' This is a nice, simple little town that is always welcoming to new members. The community regularly gathers in a specific building known as ''The Community House'', where wanderers can live together and help each other out. During their meetings, they discuss how to welcome new people and talk about ways to improve their town and make life better for everyone. === Kalabarta === * '''Population:''' 17 members. * '''Description:''' This group serves as the primary merchants and traders of The Park. They spend their time searching through the level's houses to find rare supplies, which they then sell or trade in exchange for big amounts of common resources or other rare items. Because of their location right next to the central plaza, they are highly influential. === Lovistico === * '''Population:''' 15 members. * '''Description:''' This group is the main rival of Kalabarta. They are constantly competing with them for control over trade and influence around The Park. Despite the ongoing dispute, they maintain a stable presence in their nearby sector. === Lancryville === * '''Population:''' 20 members. * '''Description:''' A very gentle and peaceful group where absolutely everyone knows each other like a big family. They are incredibly welcoming to outsiders and actively ask new wanderers to join their community and settle down with them. They also have a strong alliance with Faulehood, frequently trading resources and sharing ideas to help both towns grow safely together. There are likely more communities and small survivor groups scattered throughout '''Level 209''', but they have not been officially found or documented yet. == Entrances & Exits == === Entrances === * Finding a rare, fully indoor section covered in bright lights and walls with a blue sky wallpaper in [[Level 9]] will eventually lead you here. * Entering a specific building in [[Level 11]] that looks like '''Level 209''' on the inside means you have successfully crossed over. * Finding a modern house randomly placed inside [[Level Pi|Level 𝝅]], [[Level 10.3|10.3]], [[Level 626|626]], or the Dead Woods of [[Level 920]] will bring you here once you step inside.
